markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Thorn of Milani](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4177)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hellbreakers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=709" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 210

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You hit a creature with the affixed weapon.

 
</row>

</column>

---

This pure white thorn glows bright with the spirit of justice. When you activate the thorn, you deal an additional 1d4 spirit damage with the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) trait. You then gain weakness 1 to unholy until the end of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

</traits>

</row>

level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs=""
>
Thorn of Milani (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 210

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You deal an additional 2d4 spirit damage and don’t gain weakness to unholy.
name: Thorn of Milani

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Unassuming Face Paint](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4178)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 211

**Price** 16 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Three Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When these drab pigments are applied to your face, you appear plain and inconspicuous for 10 minutes. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks and DCs while blending into a crowd during this time. If you take a hostile action, the effects of _unassuming face_ paint immediately end.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Unassuming Face Paint

The spell attack modifier of any spell cast by Activating this item is +8, and the spell DC is 18.
  • **Armor** You gain resistance 2 to fire and unholy.
  • **Weapon** After you cast a spell by Activating the _devilwing badge_, your Strikes with the weapon deal an additional 1d4 spirit damage until the end of your next turn.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_divine lance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498). Devilwing Badge **Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 211 **Price** 90 gp Devilwing Badge (Greater) **Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 211 **Price** 425 gp --- Resistance when affixed to armor is 5, extra damage when affixed to a weapon is 1d6, the spell attack modifier is +14, and the spell DC is 24. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_crisis of faith_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1478). Devilwing Badge (Major) **Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 211 **Price** 1,750 gp --- Resistance when affixed to armor is 10, extra damage when affixed to a weapon is 1d8, the spell attack modifier is +19, and the spell DC is 29. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 4th-rank [_crisis of faith_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1478). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_divine wrath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1499).
name: Devilwing Badge

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Hellhusk Shroud](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4180)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hellbreakers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 211

**Price** 230 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This hooded cloak is popular among those who must evade or surveil fiendish authorities. The grisly secrets behind this garment belies its innocuous appearance; its fabrics include threads woven from the hair, blood, and scales of various devils. When invested, a _hellhusk shroud_ doesn't appear to radiate a magic aura to [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) or similar spells unless the spells are 4th rank or higher. As long as you're wearing the cloak, you can read and speak [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117).

**Activate—Horned Husk** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The cloak wraps around you, becoming a second skin that suffuses you with Hell's corrosive brimstone. Your appearance becomes that of a nondescript low-level devil, and you gain a +1 status bonus to your Deception checks when you [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) to maintain your disguise. To creatures with [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74), you also smell like a devil.

**Activate—Hellish Senses** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You pull your hood up, allowing the remnants of fallen devils to nestle closely and whisper Hell's secrets. You gain fiendsense as an imprecise sense within 60 feet, allowing you to sense creatures with the fiend trait as a [vague sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=2408). This effect lasts for 10 minutes or until you take this action again

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hellhusk Shroud

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Lazybones Pendant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4181)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hellbreakers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 211

**Price** 130 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _lazybones pendant_ is a six-sided die pendant carved from the regretful bones of Work-Too-Hard Whappa, a goblin unusually industrious in life and said to be making up for a long-overdue rest in his afterlife. Depicted on the five sides of this six-sided die are representations of Whappa engaged in various acts of slothful mischief, while the remaining side is utterly blank; apparently, he's so engrossed in his indolence that even his image has forgotten to show up on that side of the die.

 When you wear and invest in this pendant, you gain a +1 item bonus to checks when you [Follow the Expert](/Actions.aspx?ID=2625).

**Activate—Can't Somone Else Do it** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_phantasmal minion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1631), summoning an ephemeral version of yourself. When you Activate the pendant and each time you Sustain the spell, your phantasmal minion gains a reaction that can be used only to [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2292) an ally. It uses your skills for any skill checks and can't Aid an attack.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lazybones Pendant

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Ring of Fair Assessment](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4182)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hellbreakers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 212

**Price** 35 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While the Kalistocrats of Druma profiteer off selling weapons to both Hellbreakers and Hellknights, they also offer options suitable for intelligence and counterintelligence efforts. This white gold ring grants you a +1 item bonus to skill checks to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2363) that's primarily numerical or mathematical in nature as well as to skill checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) and [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394) to convince others of the fairness or accuracy of your judgments.

**Activate—Fair Assessment** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The Prophecies of Kalistrade reveal the secrets of wealth to you; this appears as a balance sheet of numbers only you can see. Choose an area of up to 1 cubic foot within 30 feet; you can estimate the value in gold pieces of any items within this area. If you attempt to estimate the value of items worn or held by a creature, you must succeed at a Society check to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2363) against their Will DC.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ring of Fair Assessment

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 212

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This exquisite scarlet robe embroidered with golden depictions of fiends and their weapons is fashioned after the garb of the cloistered Sisters of the Golden Erinys. The robe's belt is embroidered to look like a snake, complete with metal fangs.

**Activate—Biting Belt** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You remove the robe's belt and snap it at an enemy within your reach. As you do, it briefly animates to bite that foe, dealing 2d8 poison damage with a basic DC 18 Fortitude save. On a critical failure, the target is also [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.

This bracelet has three charms depicting a dagger, a shield, and a rose. While wearing this bracelet, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) checks. Whenever you roll a success to free someone from manacles, it counts as two successes (three on a critical success).

**Activate—No Chains** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A creature attempts to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) you; **Requirements** You are aware of the creature and aren't [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58); **Effect** The bracelet yanks your body in one direction, attempting to throw off your attacker's grip. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your Fortitude DC against the triggering Grapple.

>
[Token of Salt and Earth](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4185)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hellbreakers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 212

**Price** 225 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This charm is usually worn around the neck or hanging from a belt. It consists of a three-part hourglass with individual glass bulbs filled with sea salts, charcoal ash, and minute slivers of silver. It grants the wearer a +1 item bonus to Stealth checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) and [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) from creatures with the [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait.

**Activate—Hide from the Hateful** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [subtle](/Traits.aspx?ID=522)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** By rotating the charm along one of its concentric rings, you become [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) to creatures with the [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600), [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=599), or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) trait (your choice) for 1 minute. If you use a hostile action, you're no longer invisible after the action is completed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Token of Salt and Earth

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Calamity](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4187)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hellbreakers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 212

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=379)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Hewn from a solid block of ensorcelled stone, this _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=379)_ is monumental in heft as well as destructive force. The weapon is named for the disbelieving leaders of the Seventh Army of Exploration, who denied the power of such a weapon and claimed their losses came from natural disasters. When wielded against creatures that are larger than you, the sword gains the [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708) trait. 

**Activate—Shatter Legions** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You smash the sword down upon the ground with the anger of a conquered land and a subjugated people. Each creature in a 30-foot cone takes 4d12 piercing damage with a DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save as the earth shatters beneath their feet. Until the beginning of your next turn, you gain the benefits of [standard cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) from the broken soil around you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Calamity

This _+1 [asp coil](/Weapons.aspx?ID=233)_ is used by many trained Sisters of the Golden Erinys. It has a deep-red hue on each segment of the blade complemented by a pitch-black pommel. Death isn't the worst outcome for someone on the receiving end of its lashes.

**Activate—Taste the Pain** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** Your last action was a Strike with the _crimson thorn_ that dealt damage to a creature;  **Effect** Attempt an Intimidation check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) the creature you dealt damage to with a +2 item bonus to the roll. You don't take any penalties to this check for not sharing a language.

This [low-grade silver shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2817) (Hardness 3, HP 12, BT 6) is equipped with a _+1 [shield boss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=396)_ emblazoned with the image of a beheaded fiend. Any creature with the [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait that wields the shield becomes [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2 and can't recover from this condition while wielding the shield.

**Activate—Silvery Backlash** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212) an attack from a creature with the [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait;  **Effect** The _fiendsbane shield's_ Hardness increases to 10, and the symbol of the beheaded demon lashes out in silvery ribbons, dealing 3d6 slashing damage (DC 19 basic Reflex save) to the creature whose attack you blocked. This damage is treated as silver for the purposes of weaknesses, resistances, and the like.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

This heavy metallic _+1 [bola](/Weapons.aspx?ID=433)_ is a favorite tool of rebels and rabble-rousers against armored opponents. Due to its weight, it has a range increment of 15 feet. When you successfully Strike a creature made of metal or wearing metal armor with this weapon, the bola attaches to the metal and the targeted creature takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speed until it spends an Interact action to remove the bola.

**Activate—Overcharge** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You overcharge the magnetic properties of the bola. You deal an extra 1d6 electricity damage with your next Strike with this weapon. On a hit, the target must also succeed at a DC 18 Reflex save or become [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 round (or clumsy 2 on a critical failure), in addition to the normal effects. If the target is wearing metal armor, they take a –2 circumstance penalty to their save.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include a conductive metal.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Magnetic Bola

**Activate—Self Sacrifice** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Trigger** An ally within 20 feet of you would take physical damage; **Requirements** The _martyr's shield_ is raised; **Effect** When you activate this shield, it magically imposes itself in front of the triggering ally and takes the damage for them with the effects of [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=5212). May contain spoilers from Hellbreakers **Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 214 **Usage** 2 hands **Bulk** 2 --- This _+1 [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2854) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [scythe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=394)_ is a weapon of intricately embellished blackened metals and polished surfaces. It was originally intended to venerate Urgathoa in her role as the herald of autumn in pre-Taldor Isger but has recently found more malevolent meaning. With the scythe in her hands, Urgathoa was said to cull the harvest for feast to usher in the cold, bleak winters. As Urgathoa's legends took a darker tone, the scythe's own legend was reclaimed to venerate Erastil and Pharasma as part of the holiday for which it now claims a name. --- **Minor Gifts:** [death gaze](/Relics.aspx?ID=218), [living death](/Relics.aspx?ID=143)
**Major Gifts:** [pierce the veil](/Relics.aspx?ID=145), [tide of death](/Relics.aspx?ID=146)
**Grand Gift:** [grim specter](/Relics.aspx?ID=147)

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hellbreakers</spoilers>

<traits>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

---

This finger-shaped crystal on a thin strip of leather bridges the worlds of life and undeath, shifting from a pale opaque pink when worn by a living creature to a deep translucent purple when worn by an undead creature. While wearing the necklace, rotten food and drink taste as if they were fresh and can be eaten with no ill effects. If you're healed by healing [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) effects, you gain void resistance 5, but if you're healed by [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) effects, you instead gain vitality resistance 5.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pallid Crystal

>

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Hellbreakers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #222: Hellbreakers](/Sources.aspx?ID=307) pg. 199

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This silver platter and cloche set is intricately carved with scenes of overflowing feast tables. When you speak the command word, a dish is created for your lips and tongue alone. Anyone else who tries to eat the summoned food is compelled to immediately vomit it back up, unchewed and undigested.

**Activate—Savory Feast** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=595), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** Speaking “savor” as you lift the lid creates a dish you've never tasted before, whether it's the unique delicacy of a far-flung region or the forbidden flesh of a sapient creature. If you spend 10 minutes consuming the entire meal, you regain 2d6+8 Hit Points and gain a +2 status bonus to saving throws against being sickened for 24 hours, but if you attempt to ingest anything else in that time—including elixirs and potions—you must first succeed at a DC 5 flat check or the action is wasted.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Platter of Putrid Dreams

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blade Launcher](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4200)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 64

**Price** 18 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A blade launcher has a bow-like assembly mounted on a handled frame. The frame can be configured to fire either a [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358), [dart](/Weapons.aspx?ID=426), [shuriken](/Weapons.aspx?ID=439), or [starknife](/Weapons.aspx?ID=399). Configuring a blade launcher requires three [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions. Once properly configured, loading an appropriate thrown weapon into a blade launcher requires a single Interact action. A blade launcher can’t fire weapons for which it’s not currently configured. A weapon fired with a blade launcher loses the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [monk](/Traits.aspx?ID=656), [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), and [versatile](/Traits.aspx?ID=724) traits, if it has them, and has a range increment of 40 feet. Due to losing the thrown weapon trait, [_returning_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2845) and most other effects that allow a weapon to return don’t function; even if a launched weapon did return, you’d still need to load it into the blade launcher with an Interact action to fire the blade launcher again.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Blade Launcher

    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bomb Launcher](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4201)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 64

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This long, hollow tube is held in two hands and braced over the shoulder. Inside, it contains a small metal basket sized to hold [alchemical bombs](/Rules.aspx?ID=2210). A chute in the top delivers an alchemical bomb into the internal basket, while a lever on the underside pulls the basket back and locks it in place. Loading an alchemical bomb into a bomb launcher requires a single [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action. With a pull of a trigger, the basket speeds forward, allowing you to [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with the loaded alchemical bomb over long distances. Bombs fired with a bomb launcher have a [range](/Rules.aspx?ID=2195) increment of 60 feet.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bomb Launcher

    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Deployable Cover](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4202)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 64

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This thick mat of canvas, foliage, and wood is mounted on a tripod of flexible metal struts, folded into a batonlike shape, and clamped shut. You can rapidly deploy it on the ground with an Interact action to create cover. Deployable cover is large enough to [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=2307) behind, allowing you (and others) to gain standard cover when you use the Take Cover action. Before it can be used again, deployable cover must be carefully folded and clamped shut, which takes 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Deployable Cover
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

Deployable Cover (Ballistic Cover)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 64

**Price** 35 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Specially crafted to protect against bullet fire, a ballistic cover also works against other physical projectiles, such as arrows, bolts, and thrown weapons. While a creature has cover from Taking [Cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) behind a ballistic cover, it gains resistance 2 to piercing damage from ranged weapons and ranged unarmed attacks.
name: Deployable Cover

Deployable cover is large enough to [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=2307) behind, allowing you (and others) to gain standard cover when you use the Take Cover action. Before it can be used again, deployable cover must be carefully folded and clamped shut, which takes 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Deployable Cover
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 64

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Deployable Cover (Ballistic Cover)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 64

**Price** 35 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Specially crafted to protect against bullet fire, a ballistic cover also works against other physical projectiles, such as arrows, bolts, and thrown weapons. While a creature has cover from Taking [Cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) behind a ballistic cover, it gains resistance 2 to piercing damage from ranged weapons and ranged unarmed attacks.
name: Deployable Cover (Ballistic Cover)

    level="1"
    right="Item 0+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Grappling Gun](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4203)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 64

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wooden, pistol-like device features a large reel coiled with 100 feet of thin metal cord and can fire a grappling hook up to 100 feet. To reload the grappling gun, you must manually recoil the cord by turning the reel’s crank, and then lock in the grappling hook. Reloading a grappling gun takes 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wrist Grappler
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Firebrands](/Sources.aspx?ID=196) pg. 82

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 1

**Bulk** 2

</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

</column>

---

This specialized [grappling gun](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1099), which attaches to your wrist, connects to a spool of 50 feet of thin metal cord attached to a pack on your back. The wrist grappler and pack are small enough that they can be concealed with a loose jacket or similar piece of clothing. You can deploy or stow the gun from the attached wrist device with an Interact action. Reloading a wrist grappler uses the same process as reloading a standard grappling gun and takes 1 minute.

pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 1

**Bulk** 2

</row>

**Access** Second-mark members of the Firebrands have access to this item.

</column>

---

Reloading a clockwork wrist grappler takes three Interact actions.

Grappling Gun

</traits>

**Price** 15 gp

---

Clockwork controls the reel on this grappling gun, reeling the grappling hook back in when you pull a lever. Reloading a clockwork grappling gun takes three [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Net Launcher](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4204)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 65

**Price** 16 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wide, hollow tube is two to three feet long and fires an unattached net at much greater range than one can be thrown. A net launcher can be wielded while propped up on your shoulder or cradled under your arm. A net must be carefully folded to be launched without tangling. Properly loading a net into a net launcher takes 1 minute. A net fired with a net launcher can target a Medium or smaller creature within 40 feet, rather than 20 feet.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Net Launcher

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wind-Up Wings](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4205)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 65

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to a thrown weapon

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These delicate clockwork wings enable thrown weapons to soar through the air at high speeds, performing turns and other aerial maneuvers midflight. Wind-up wings also have different types of special effects depending on the type. Wind-up wings must be attached to a [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=711) weapon and wound to function. A thrown weapon with an attached set of wind-up wings can’t have anything else attached to it, or the wings cease to function.

Attaching or detaching a pair of wind-up wings to a thrown weapon requires a [repair kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2747), and the process takes 10 minutes. Winding an attached pair of wind-up wings takes three [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions. There are a variety of wind-up wings, with different effects.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wind-Up Wings (Flutterback)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 65

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you make a thrown [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with the weapon to which a pair of flutterback wind-up wings are attached, and the wings are wound, the wings fly the weapon back to your hand after the Strike is complete. If your hands are full when the weapon returns, the wings hover in place three feet above the ground. At the end of your turn, the wings are wound down; they don’t function again until wound. If you aren’t holding the weapon when the flutterback wind-up wings become wound down, the weapon falls to the ground.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wind-Up Wings (Homing)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 65

**Price** 1,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you make a thrown Strike with the weapon to which a pair of homing wind-up wings are attached, and the wings are wound, the wings seek out your target, flying through cover and avoiding obstacles. You ignore the target’s [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition and reduce the target’s cover by one step (lesser cover to no cover, standard cover to lesser cover, or greater cover to standard cover). After the Strike is complete, the wings are wound down; they don’t function again until wound.
name: Wind-Up Wings

These delicate clockwork wings enable thrown weapons to soar through the air at high speeds, performing turns and other aerial maneuvers midflight. …

summary_markdown: These delicate clockwork wings enable thrown weapons to soar through the air at high speeds, performing turns and other aerial maneuvers midflight. …

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wind-Up Wings](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4205)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 65

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to a thrown weapon

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These delicate clockwork wings enable thrown weapons to soar through the air at high speeds, performing turns and other aerial maneuvers midflight. Wind-up wings also have different types of special effects depending on the type. Wind-up wings must be attached to a [thrown](/Traits.aspx?ID=711) weapon and wound to function. A thrown weapon with an attached set of wind-up wings can’t have anything else attached to it, or the wings cease to function.

Attaching or detaching a pair of wind-up wings to a thrown weapon requires a [repair kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2747), and the process takes 10 minutes. Winding an attached pair of wind-up wings takes three [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions. There are a variety of wind-up wings, with different effects.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wind-Up Wings (Flutterback)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 65

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you make a thrown [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with the weapon to which a pair of flutterback wind-up wings are attached, and the wings are wound, the wings fly the weapon back to your hand after the Strike is complete. If your hands are full when the weapon returns, the wings hover in place three feet above the ground. At the end of your turn, the wings are wound down; they don’t function again until wound. If you aren’t holding the weapon when the flutterback wind-up wings become wound down, the weapon falls to the ground.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wind-Up Wings (Homing)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 65

**Price** 1,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you make a thrown Strike with the weapon to which a pair of homing wind-up wings are attached, and the wings are wound, the wings seek out your target, flying through cover and avoiding obstacles. You ignore the target’s [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition and reduce the target’s cover by one step (lesser cover to no cover, standard cover to lesser cover, or greater cover to standard cover). After the Strike is complete, the wings are wound down; they don’t function again until wound.
name: Wind-Up Wings (Flutterback)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ablative Armor Plating](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4206)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 66

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to armor or traveler's clothing

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Ablative armor plating is additional plating, combined with springs, clever clockwork, and improvised padding, to absorb damage by knocking the additional pieces free. The pieces slowly come apart on their own with time and movement. The process of attaching the plating takes 10 minutes and grants the wearer of the armor the listed [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) for the stated duration or until lost. Removing the plating early destroys it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

---

The wearer gains 5 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute or until lost.

Ablative armor plating is additional plating, combined with springs, clever clockwork, and improvised padding, to absorb damage by knocking the additional pieces free. The pieces slowly come apart on their own with time and movement. The process of attaching the plating takes 10 minutes and grants the wearer of the armor the listed [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) for the stated duration or until lost. Removing the plating early destroys it.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 67

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to a shield

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Ablative shield plating is an amalgam device that provides a layer of additional protection for a shield. Ablative shield plating is cobbled together from parts of other shields, wires, scraps, and bits of detritus, adding mass to a shield. The process of attaching ablative shield plating takes 10 minutes, and you can’t attach ablative shield plating to a shield that has any other attached item, such as a [shield boss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=396) or [shield spikes](/Weapons.aspx?ID=397). When the shield is damaged, this additional material crumples, breaks, and falls apart, absorbing some of the energy of the blow. Otherwise, the additional material slowly crumbles over time. Applying the plating grants the shield [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for the stated duration or until lost. Removing the plating early destroys it.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ablative Shield Plating](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4207)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 67

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** attached to a shield

**Bulk** 1
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Ablative shield plating is an amalgam device that provides a layer of additional protection for a shield. Ablative shield plating is cobbled together from parts of other shields, wires, scraps, and bits of detritus, adding mass to a shield. The process of attaching ablative shield plating takes 10 minutes, and you can’t attach ablative shield plating to a shield that has any other attached item, such as a [shield boss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=396) or [shield spikes](/Weapons.aspx?ID=397). When the shield is damaged, this additional material crumples, breaks, and falls apart, absorbing some of the energy of the blow. Otherwise, the additional material slowly crumbles over time. Applying the plating grants the shield [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for the stated duration or until lost. Removing the plating early destroys it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 67

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The shield gains 5 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 minute or until lost.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blast Boots](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4208)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 67

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn (attached to footwear or feet)

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These sets of rockets come in pairs and strap onto existing footwear (or a creature’s feet). Inserting them and aligning them properly takes 1 minute. When you Activate the blast boots, you can [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378), without the need to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) first. Higher-level versions increase the distance of your High Jump or Long Jump.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blast Boots (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 67

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blast Boots (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 67

**Price** 11 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you Activate the moderate blast boots to High Jump, you can increase the vertical distance of your High Jump by up to 30 feet. When you Activate them to Long Jump, you can increase the horizontal distance of your Long Jump by up to 45 feet.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blast Boots (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 67

**Price** 140 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you Activate the greater blast boots to High Jump, you can increase the vertical distance of your High Jump by up to 50 feet. When you Activate them to Long Jump, you can increase the horizontal distance of your Long Jump by up to 75 feet. Additionally, for 1 minute, the greater blast boots continue to boost your jumps. Every time you Leap during the duration, you can move 30 feet in any direction, or your normal Leap distance, whichever is further.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blast Boots (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 67

**Price** 2,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you Activate the major blast boots to High Jump, you can increase the vertical distance of your High Jump by up to 80 feet. When you Activate them to Long Jump, you can increase the horizontal distance of your Long Jump by up to 120 feet. Additionally, for 1 minute, the major blast boots remain active, allowing you to clumsily fly through the air for the duration. You gain a 30-foot [Fly speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) for the duration, but any time you [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), you are [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 until the start of your next turn.
name: Blast Boots

When you Activate the moderate blast boots to High Jump, you can increase the vertical distance of your High Jump by up to 30 feet. When you Activate them to Long Jump, you can increase the horizontal distance of your Long Jump by up to 45 feet.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blast Boots (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 67

**Price** 140 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you Activate the greater blast boots to High Jump, you can increase the vertical distance of your High Jump by up to 50 feet. When you Activate them to Long Jump, you can increase the horizontal distance of your Long Jump by up to 75 feet. Additionally, for 1 minute, the greater blast boots continue to boost your jumps. Every time you Leap during the duration, you can move 30 feet in any direction, or your normal Leap distance, whichever is further.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Blast Boots (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 67

**Price** 2,750 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you Activate the major blast boots to High Jump, you can increase the vertical distance of your High Jump by up to 80 feet. When you Activate them to Long Jump, you can increase the horizontal distance of your Long Jump by up to 120 feet. Additionally, for 1 minute, the major blast boots remain active, allowing you to clumsily fly through the air for the duration. You gain a 30-foot [Fly speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) for the duration, but any time you [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), you are [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 until the start of your next turn.
name: Blast Boots (Greater)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cauterizing Torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4209)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 67

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small, clockwork torch device uses a trigger-operated sparker to ignite a flow of flammable gas, creating a short, hot flame capable of rapidly cauterizing wounds and helping to stop bleeding. The cauterizing torch is applied to yourself or an adjacent target. The target attempts an immediate [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to end any [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) effect with the lower DC for particularly effective assistance.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cauterizing Torch

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Chameleon Suit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4210)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 67

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn over armor and clothes

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Characters from Absalom, New Thassilon, Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, or especially technological regions of Arcadia like the Deadshot Lands have access to uncommon clockwork items from the first two chapters of this book. Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This complex clockwork outerwear, worn over armor or other clothing, is composed of thin, interlocking scales of myriad colors attached to numerous clockwork gears that can rotate between all visible colors and shades.

When activated, the clockwork gears work double-time to temporarily switch and adjust the scales to match the exact coloration of the area around you, allowing you to blend in as long as you stay still. For the duration, you can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) without needing [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) or concealment to do so. This doesn’t allow you to [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) without ending your movement in cover or concealment, however, as the clockwork flips over and adjusts the scales to match your background as you move, giving the impression of rippling waves of color and revealing your movement. Once the duration expires, the overworked clockwork and scales fall apart.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Chameleon Suit
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 67

**Price** 16 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The effect lasts for 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Chameleon Suit (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 68

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The effect lasts for 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Chameleon Suit (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 68

**Price** 320 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The effect lasts for 1 hour.
name: Chameleon Suit

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Clockwork Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4211)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 68

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Characters from Absalom, New Thassilon, Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, or especially technological regions of Arcadia like the Deadshot Lands have access to uncommon clockwork items from the first two chapters of this book. Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This heavy set of mechanical goggles contains a variety of different lenses for different situations, including lenses that filter light at different intensities and frequencies and even some that capture heat and other stimuli. Clockwork mechanisms can swap between the lenses rapidly as needed. However, many of these lenses can only capture a single stimulus once and then are forever etched with it, making them unsuitable for long-term use. When activated, the clockwork mechanism on the goggles rapidly switches between the different lenses, granting you the benefits of all of them for a brief period of time.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, you gain [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411) for 10 minutes.

level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Clockwork Goggles (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, you gain low-light vision and [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) for 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Clockwork Goggles (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 68

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, you gain low-light vision, darkvision, and the effects of [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) for 10 minutes.
name: Clockwork Goggles

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cryomister](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4212)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 68

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you Activate a cryomister, you can either place it in an adjacent square or toss it up to 20 feet away. Once you’ve done so, the metallic device instantly disperses a heavy mist, settling quickly on nearby flat surfaces and forming a sheet of ice. The cryomister creates a [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) with a radius depending on the type, making the affected area difficult terrain for 1 minute; , at the GM’s discretion, however, this duration might be reduced in unusually hot conditions or increased in unusually cold ones. If an affected square takes fire damage, the [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) is removed. Though not designed as a weapon, the cryomister’s rapidly cooling mist deals cold splash damage depending on the type to creatures in the area on activation. Creatures attempting to move through the affected area can attempt an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) check with a DC depending on the type to [ignore the difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) and move at their normal Speed. Throwing the cryomister onto the surface of a liquid creates a floating piece of ice for the same duration, capable of supporting one or more creatures of the listed size.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Cryomister (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 68

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The area is a 5-foot burst, the Acrobatics DC is 17, and the cold splash damage is 1. The floating ice can support one creature up to Medium size.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Cryomister (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 68

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The area is a 10-foot burst, the Acrobatics DC is 21, and the cold splash damage is 3. The floating ice can support one Large creature or two Medium or smaller creatures.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Cryomister (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 68

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The area is a 15-foot burst, the Acrobatics DC is 30, and the cold splash damage is 5. The floating ice can support one Huge creature, two Large creatures, or four Medium or smaller creatures.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Cryomister (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 68

**Price** 2,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The area is a 20-foot burst, the Acrobatics DC is 39, and the cold splash damage is 8. The floating ice can support one Gargantuan creature, two Huge creatures, four Large creatures, or eight Medium or smaller creatures. The ice can’t support Gargantuan creatures larger than a 20-by-20-foot space, such as a mu spore.
name: Cryomister

The ice can’t support Gargantuan creatures larger than a 20-by-20-foot space, such as a mu spore.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cryomister](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4212)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 68

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you Activate a cryomister, you can either place it in an adjacent square or toss it up to 20 feet away. Once you’ve done so, the metallic device instantly disperses a heavy mist, settling quickly on nearby flat surfaces and forming a sheet of ice. The cryomister creates a [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) with a radius depending on the type, making the affected area difficult terrain for 1 minute; , at the GM’s discretion, however, this duration might be reduced in unusually hot conditions or increased in unusually cold ones. If an affected square takes fire damage, the [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) is removed. Though not designed as a weapon, the cryomister’s rapidly cooling mist deals cold splash damage depending on the type to creatures in the area on activation. Creatures attempting to move through the affected area can attempt an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) check with a DC depending on the type to [ignore the difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) and move at their normal Speed. Throwing the cryomister onto the surface of a liquid creates a floating piece of ice for the same duration, capable of supporting one or more creatures of the listed size.

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 68 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_ **Usage** worn **Bulk** 1 **Activate**Interact --- This rare gadget uses Stasian technology to grant someone a burst of activity, though its use can be painful. The electromuscular stimulator must be carefully attached to you, requiring 1 minute to do so. You can attach an electromuscular stimulator to yourself. When you Activate an attached electromuscular stimulator, roll a [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) check, using the Crafting modifier of the creature who attached the stimulator to you, with a DC equal to the standard DC for your level. The effects of the activation depend on the result of the Crafting check. **Critical Success** You gain the [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) condition for 1 minute and can use the extra action each round only to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) or [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306).
**Success** As critical success, but you also take 3 [persistent electricity damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).
**Failure** You gain the quickened condition for 2 rounds and can use the extra action each round only to Stride or Strike. You take 3 persistent electricity damage.
**Critical Failure** You take 2d6 electrical damage and 3 persistent electricity damage.
name: Electromuscular Stimulator

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An etheric essence disruptor emits bursts of discordant ethereal energy through a process based upon Stasian etheric-spirit singers, though without the need for a Stasian coil. Etheric essence disruptors attempt to break down the workings of a spell that manipulates spiritual essence. Upon activation, attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) an active [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) or [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) spell within the listed range, with the listed counteract modifier and counteract level.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Explosive Mine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4215)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 69

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you Activate an explosive mine, you place it on the ground in a square within your reach, priming it to explode when a creature steps on that square. If you have time in advance to prepare, you can place the mine on the ground before activating it, and you or an ally can try to use [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) to [Conceal an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=2403) before you Activate the mine. If you don’t Conceal the mine, its position is obvious at a glance. 

Once activated, the mine is primed to explode when enough pressure is placed on the square where it’s located. Typically, this occurs when a Small or larger creature moves onto the square, though it could happen if a creature intentionally places pressure on the square from a distance to safely trigger the mine. Once triggered, the mine explodes, dealing the listed fire damage to any creatures in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Explosive Mine (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 69

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The mine deals 2d6 fire damage, and the Reflex DC is 15.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Explosive Mine (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 69

**Price** 40 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The mine deals 6d6 fire damage, and the Reflex DC is 20.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Explosive Mine (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 69

**Price** 400 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The mine deals 12d6 fire damage, and the Reflex DC is 29.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Explosive Mine (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 69

**Price** 4,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The mine deals 18d6 fire damage in a 10-foot emanation, and the Reflex DC is 38.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Explosive Mine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4215)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 69

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When you Activate an explosive mine, you place it on the ground in a square within your reach, priming it to explode when a creature steps on that square. If you have time in advance to prepare, you can place the mine on the ground before activating it, and you or an ally can try to use [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) to [Conceal an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=2403) before you Activate the mine. If you don’t Conceal the mine, its position is obvious at a glance. 

Once activated, the mine is primed to explode when enough pressure is placed on the square where it’s located. Typically, this occurs when a Small or larger creature moves onto the square, though it could happen if a creature intentionally places pressure on the square from a distance to safely trigger the mine. Once triggered, the mine explodes, dealing the listed fire damage to any creatures in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Flare Beacon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4216)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 69

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Flare beacons create an incredibly bright light for a brief period of time. They are often used to signal others to the beacon’s location, to coordinate assaults, to request rescue, or for other similar reasons. Higher-level beacons have a radius so large that they can be seen from miles away at night. When you Activate a flare beacon, you can either place it on the ground in a space within your reach or toss it up to 60 feet straight up. The beacon then sparks into being, casting [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) in a 20-foot radius and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) in the next 20 feet for 1 minute. A flare beacon in the air falls at a rate of 10 feet per round. Creatures adjacent to a flare beacon must succeed at a DC 15 Fortitude save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until they are no longer adjacent to it.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Flare Beacon (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 70

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Flare Beacon (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 70

**Price** 35 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The beacon shines bright light in a 60-foot radius and dim light in the next 60 feet. Creatures within a 10-foot radius of the beacon must succeed at a DC 20 Fortitude save or be dazzled until they are no longer within 10 feet of it.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Flare Beacon (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 70

**Price** 180 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The beacon shines bright light in a 100-foot radius and dim light in the next 100 feet. Creatures within a 20-foot radius of the beacon must succeed at a DC 27 Fortitude save or be dazzled until they are no longer within 20 feet of it. Additionally, on a critical failure, they are also [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Flare Beacon (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 70

**Price** 1,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The beacon shines bright light in a 180-foot radius and dim light in the next 180 feet. Creatures within a 30-foot radius of the beacon must succeed at a DC 35 Fortitude save or be dazzled until they are no longer within 30 feet of it. Additionally, on a critical failure, they are also blinded for 1 round.
name: Flare Beacon

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Flare beacons create an incredibly bright light for a brief period of time. They are often used to signal others to the beacon’s location, to coordinate assaults, to request rescue, or for other similar reasons. Higher-level beacons have a radius so large that they can be seen from miles away at night. When you Activate a flare beacon, you can either place it on the ground in a space within your reach or toss it up to 60 feet straight up. The beacon then sparks into being, casting [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) in a 20-foot radius and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) in the next 20 feet for 1 minute. A flare beacon in the air falls at a rate of 10 feet per round. Creatures adjacent to a flare beacon must succeed at a DC 15 Fortitude save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until they are no longer adjacent to it.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Gadget Skates](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4217)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Clockwork" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=331" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 70

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

**Price** 21 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn (attached to footwear or feet)

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Characters from Absalom, New Thassilon, Alkenstar, Dongun Hold, or especially technological regions of Arcadia like the Deadshot Lands have access to uncommon clockwork items from the first two chapters of this book. Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact ([move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Gadget skates are metal devices that come in pairs and strap onto existing footwear (or a creature’s feet).

When you Activate gadget skates, clockwork gears reveal small wheels that propel you along the ground, moving you up to twice your Speed. During this movement, you must succeed at a DC 20 [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) check to turn up to 90 degrees, and you can’t turn more than once or more than 90 degrees. During the activation and after the activation is complete, you can’t [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304), or otherwise move on the ground until the end of your next turn. However, you can use this movement instead of a Stride to perform a [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2377) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=2378) just after the activation ends, in the direction of your last movement using the gadget skates.

You can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2631) the Activation of your gadget skates for up to 1 minute. Starting on the turn after you Activate the gadget skates, the first time you Sustain the Activation each turn, you gain the effects of the activation again: you move up to twice your Speed in the same direction as your last movement with the gadget skates, and the restrictions continue to apply until you choose to cease Sustaining the Activation or you reach the maximum duration. Once the effect ends, the skates fall away and you can move normally.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gadget Skates

Gecko pads are thin, clockwork devices shaped like palms, which can be strapped onto existing handwear or a creature’s hands. The pads give you a [climb Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2349) equal to your Speed as long as your hands are free.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Impact Foam Chassis](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4219)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 70

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All Pathfinder Society agents have access to all uncommon clockwork items and gadgets from Chapters 1 and 2 of this book_

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn over armor and clothes

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access**  Due to the use of technology, all gadgets are uncommon or rare, though inventors and regions with access to inventors have access to uncommon gadgets.

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>see below</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While you’re wearing an impact foam chassis wrapped around your body, it Activates automatically whenever you [fall](/Rules.aspx?ID=2352) at least 10 feet, causing a layer of soft, flexible impact foam to expand beneath and around you to cushion the fall. The impact foam decreases the falling damage by up to the listed amount, taking damage equal to the amount of falling damage the foam reduced. If this is enough to destroy the foam, it disperses immediately. However, if the foam has any Hit Points remaining, it remains surrounding you for up to 1 minute. While you’re surrounded in foam, you are [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) and have standard cover against other creatures, and other creatures have standard cover against you. The foam has an [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) DC of 15; a creature can also remove it by spending three [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions. You or others can also attack the foam (the foam has an AC of 12), which disperses if reduced to 0 Hit Points.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Impact Foam Chassis (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Guns & Gears](/Sources.aspx?ID=318) pg. 70

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A lesser impact foam chassis prevents up to 20 falling damage and has 20 Hit Points.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Impact Foam Chassis (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>
